title: commands_detect.mjs: detect host-project standard commands from manifests
action: Create templates/lib/commands_detect.mjs exporting detectCommands(root) that scans package.json scripts, Makefile targets, pyproject.toml, composer.json, *.csproj, and go.mod+Makefile (per D3) and returns [{key, value, source}] candidates for the COMMAND_KEYS from lib/state.mjs (setup/start/test/verify). Mapping rule: key = exact-name match of a script/target against COMMAND_KEYS (package.json scripts, Makefile targets, composer.json scripts); value is always the invocable command, never the recipe body — npm run <script> (npm test for test), make <target>, composer <script>. Ecosystem conventions fire only on marker files with no explicit script match: pyproject.toml -> test: pytest; *.csproj -> test: dotnet test; go.mod -> test: go test ./... — convention candidates carry the marker file as source so the user sees they are conventions to confirm, which is why this does not violate never-invent (propose-only; user confirms per D3). Dedup: at most one candidate per key; priority = the source order listed above (package.json first), within a glob lexicographic first filename wins. Pure read: manifest files only, never .env/secret-shaped paths. The module is dual-use: exports detectCommands AND carries a guarded CLI entry (run-directly check via import.meta.url) that prints the JSON candidate list for the repo root argument or cwd — this is the invocable surface exploring/onboarding name in later sessions (node .bee/bin/lib/commands_detect.mjs). Add fixture-dir unit tests to templates/tests/test_lib.mjs: empty repo returns [], package.json mapping, Makefile targets, package.json-beats-Makefile dedup, and one convention source (pyproject or csproj). Windows-safe absolute paths only (critical-pattern 20260708).

truths: detectCommands on a repo with no manifests returns an empty array without throwing
Every returned candidate carries key from COMMAND_KEYS, a non-empty value, and the source file it came from
package.json and Makefile both defining the same key yields exactly one candidate per the dedup rule
Running commands_detect.mjs directly prints the JSON candidates for a target root (CLI entry), while importing it stays side-effect-free
key_links: commands_detect.mjs imports COMMAND_KEYS from ./state.mjs — no duplicated key list
prohibitions: No writes to .bee/config.json from the detector (propose-only per D3)
No reading of secret-shaped files (.env*, *.pem, credentials*)

trace.outcome: commands_detect.mjs created: detectCommands(root) scans package.json/Makefile/pyproject.toml/composer.json/*.csproj/go.mod per D3, returns [{key,value,source}] deduped one-per-key (explicit sources beat conventions, package.json first), imports COMMAND_KEYS from ./state.mjs, guarded CLI entry prints JSON; propose-only, never writes config; 6 fixture tests added to test_lib.mjs
